text.title: Rate Floor Output Contract
text.summary: JSON Schema for the RateFloor artefact this methodology produces. The validator enforces this contract before the floor drives a quote.

example.code: { "floor_usd_per_hour": 145, "sensitivity": [ {"band": "low", "billable_pct": 0.45, "floor_usd_per_hour": 178}, {"band": "baseline", "billable_pct": 0.55, "floor_usd_per_hour": 145}, {"band": "high", "billable_pct": 0.65, "floor_usd_per_hour": 123} ], "project_minimum_usd": 2900, "breakdown": { "target_income_loaded": 144000, "tax_load_pct": 0.34, "working_weeks": 46, "billable_pct": 0.55, "benefit_cost_usd": 8000, "tooling_cost_usd": 3000, "profit_margin_pct": 0.20 }, "defense_narrative": "Loaded rate accounting for downtime, taxes, tooling, and a 20% reinvestment margin standard for senior independent practice.", "inputs_warning": [], "computed_at": "2026-05-23" }

example.note: tax_load too low, working_weeks > 46, billable_pct > 0.55, margin < 0.15, sensitivity bands missing, narrative leaks personal income.

forbidden.pattern: breakdown.billable_pct > 0.55 without inputs_warning referencing historical-data source
sensitivity bands missing or not at ±10pp billable
defense_narrative leaks raw income / personal expenses (tokens: salary, my income, my mortgage, my expenses)

allowed-transformations.transformation: Convert to non-USD currency at current rate for non-US operators (annotate currency).
Drop benefits row in jurisdictions where benefits are state-funded (NHS, etc.).
